statement.p: An object was launched from the top of a hill with an upward vertical velocity of 120 feet per second. The height of the object can be modeled by the function h(t)={-16t^{2}+120t+250}\text{,} where t represents the number of seconds after the launch. Assume the object landed on the ground at sea level. Find the answer using graphing technology.
Use a table to list the objects height within the first second after it was launched, at an increment of 0.1 second. Fill in the blanks. Round your answers to two decimal places when needed.

solution.p: Define the function h(t)={-16t^{2}+120t+250} with technology. Set the tables starting value to 0\text{,} and use an increment of 0.1\text{.}
